name: Chinese Fruited Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 lb Boneless pork, cut into 3/4-inch strips
1/4 cup Dorothy Lynch Home Style or Reduced Calorie dressing
1 package Frozen pea pods, slightly thawed (6 oz)
1 can Apricot halves, drained and Sliced, reserving syrup (16 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pork in a bowl.
2. Combine soy sauce, dressing and 3 tablespoons cornstarch and pour over meat. Allow to marinate for 15 minutes.
3. Heat 4 tablespoons oil in a wok or large skillet.
4. Drain the pork, reserving the marinade. Add pork to the wok and cook over high heat, stirring about 4-5 minutes or until meat is done.
5. Remove meat and set aside.
6. Drain the wok.
7. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the wok.
8. Add snow peas and stir-fry for about 30 seconds.
9. Add water, cover and steam for about 30 seconds.
10. Stir in pork and apricots.
11. Dissolve cornstarch in the reserved apricot syrup. Blend in the remaining marinade.
12. Add to wok and heat until sauce thickens.
13. Serve each portion with half a cup of hot cooked rice.
